title: Package Layout, Pyproject Metadata, Entry Points, and Reproducible Builds · Program Brief: Entry Points
summary: Bookbot works only from the repository root, imports an uninstalled local file, lacks metadata and version identity, builds from a dirty tree, and publishes a wheel never tested after installation. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the entry points increment in installable reproducible Bookbot package. Learner…
objectives: Support python -m execution and an installed console script that call the same testable main boundary.
Declare canonical name, version policy, Python requirement, description, license, authorship, classifiers, URLs, and build system in pyproject.toml.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-entry-points — Support python -m execution and an installed console script that call the same testable main boundary.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-packaging-release-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-packaging-release-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_packaging_release_4(metadata_fields: int = 9, artifact_count: int = 2, clean_revision: bool = True, contents_inspected: bool = True, fresh_install_passed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if metadata_fields < 6 or artifact_count < 2:
        return False, artifact_count, "package-shape"
    if not clean_revision:
        return False, artifact_count, "dirty-build"
    if not contents_inspected:
        return False, artifact_count, "unknown-contents"
    if not fresh_install_passed:
        return False, artifact_count, "install-failure"
    assert artifact_count >= 2
    return True, metadata_fields, "installable-artifacts"

Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-entry-points-1mhnln
# Competency: bookbot-entry-points.
# Case 5935: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable.
def evidence_bookbot_entry_points_1mhnln(metadata_fields: int = 9, artifact_count: int = 2, clean_revision: bool = True, contents_inspected: bool = True, fresh_install_passed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1mhnln = "1mhnln-nlnhm1-5935"
    assert evidence_variant_1mhnln
    evidence_axes_1mhnln = (
        "corpus1mhnln",
        "policynlnhm1",
        "revision5935",
        "command1mhnln5935",
        "reportnlnhm15935",
        "test59351mhnln",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1mhnln)) == 6
    if metadata_fields < 6 or artifact_count < 2:
        return False, artifact_count, "package-shape"
    if not clean_revision:
        return False, artifact_count, "dirty-build"
    if not contents_inspected:
        return False, artifact_count, "unknown-contents"
    if not fresh_install_passed:
        return False, artifact_count, "install-failure"
    assert artifact_count >= 2
    return True, metadata_fields, "installable-artifacts"

Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Support python -m execution and an installed console script that call the same testable main boundary.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Entry Points model · Theory Entry Points, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: __main__.py and console_scripts must contain separate command implementations.
